acceptance_criteria: Backend deployment posture is expressed as wasmCloud on Wasmtime with deployable component metadata instead of native service binaries.
The OS shell subset is rehomed under ui/ and exposes only the governed release apps for this product profile.
SurrealDB access is centralized behind shared repository abstractions and UI data access stays on typed SDK/contracts.
Workspace tooling, CI, and process validation encode the new ui and wasmCloud-first rules.

open_questions.question: Nomad host/service topology can be refined further once real lattice rollout automation is introduced.

objective: Define the runtime, data, and UI boundaries for the integrated architecture.
design_summary: Treat services/workflows as pure Rust business crates with adjacent wasmCloud component descriptors.
Make ui/ the only owner of Leptos/Tauri models and drive governed data through platform/sdk snapshots and commands.
Centralize SurrealDB access in shared/surrealdb-access and use Wasmtime as the default strategy runtime.
public_interface_changes: Add schemas/wit/v1 plus generated wasmCloud binding constants and lattice configuration shapes.
Expand platform/sdk with typed UI dashboard, command, query, and event transport contracts.
Expose Control Center as the new release-visible status surface inside ui/.
boundary_impacts: services/ and workflows/ now surface adjacent deployable component metadata.
Nomad manifests pivot from raw service binaries to lattice support services and hosts.
process-method now enforces explicit ui profile evidence.
